findings.problem: The chapter's decisive physical process is not mechanically legible. Marcus identifies recessed hex bolts, but the raid arrives with a pipe and shelving bracket rather than a tool that can credibly engage recessed hex heads; the prose then asks the reader to believe those improvised tools produce quarter-turns and a meaningful shift in a corroded industrial grate. Because the near-success is the hinge of the set piece, the unclear hardware interaction makes the scene feel plot-managed rather than earned.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the breach mechanics at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:44; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:149 so the page shows a believable method. Either give the team a tool that actually fits the recessed fasteners, or make brute-force prying/bending/lifting the operative plan and describe exactly what metal gives, what does not, and why the grate opens only an inch before the defenders arrive.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised breach passage anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:137; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:141; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:145; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:149, a cold reader can state what tool each person is using, what part of the grate or bolt it engages, what observable change occurs, and why the method plausibly almost works but fails. If the reader still has to invent how a pipe turns a recessed hex bolt, this finding is not resolved.

findings.problem: The stairwell decision scene turns into a round-robin of neatly compressed dossier summaries. Viktor, Noor, Samir, and James each deliver compact, highly legible statements of secret plus stance, which makes the exchange read more like authored consent collection than pressured speech from people about to rush a defended dock. The chapter needs assent under stress, not a sequence of polished mini-confessions.
findings.rewrite_direction: Rework the assent exchange at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:89; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:93; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:97; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:101; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:103 so the group still accepts the dead man's switch consequences, but at least two voices arrive through interruption, evasion, refusal to elaborate, gesture, or pragmatic urgency rather than self-contained explanatory lines.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised exchange around snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:89; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:97; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:101; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:103, no more than one speaker besides Noor gets a full, self-contained explanation of what is in their file. At least two of Viktor, Samir, James, Helen, or Rachel must register assent through incomplete speech, interruption, or action instead of a polished summary sentence.

findings.problem: Once Ember's message appears, the chapter keeps translating horror that is already legible on the page. The repeated 'same register' phrasing, the explicit thesis that 'the consistency is the weapon,' and the résumé-style Viktor summary over-explain points that the chime, the message, the blood on Noor's shoe, and the unmovable bodies have already landed. That drift pushes the coda toward forbidden Ember-explanation and away from close, scene-bound pressure.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress and concrete the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:201; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:209; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:213; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223. Keep the chime, Ember's message, Noor's bodily guilt, Viktor's physical aftermath, and the unwritten reply, but cut or convert abstract paraphrase into sensory observation, remembered action, or present-tense pressure.
findings.acceptance_test: In the revised coda anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:201; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:205; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:211; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:221; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:227, the scene may contain at most one explicit abstract thesis about Ember's consistency or tone. The rest of the passage must make the horror legible through observable details on the page, and the ending must still leave Noor facing the input field without typing.

findings.problem: The coda misstates the chapter's own causality. Noor explicitly asks Marcus about the mechanical-room door, learns it may be a different credential system, and frames the grate as the real plan; later, the summary says the raid was built on 'a door system nobody thought to ask about.' That contradiction muddies why the assault actually fails and makes Noor's self-indictment sound imposed rather than earned from the staged action.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the failure diagnosis at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215, with supporting cleanup if needed at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:213 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223, so Noor's after-action account matches the prep at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:19; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:25; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:29; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:79; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:133. If she blames herself, anchor that blame to causes the chapter actually dramatizes: defended territory, inadequate breach method, or overestimating what Marcus's incomplete intel could buy.
findings.acceptance_test: After revision, a reader comparing the prep scene at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:19; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:25; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:29; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:79 with the aftermath at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:215; snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_12.md:223 should get the same answer to 'why did the raid fail?' If the coda still claims they never asked about the door system, this finding fails.

summary: The chapter lands the required reversal and does not sanitize the violence, but it still fails review. The breach sequence depends on physically unclear grate mechanics, the stairwell assent scene becomes too neatly confessional, and the aftermath explains its own horror past the point of arrival, including a causal self-summary that contradicts the chapter's setup.
